### The Congruence Generated by a Relation

This is the [Setoid.Algebras.Congruences.Generation][] module of the [Agda Universal Algebra Library][].

The [Setoid.Algebras.Congruences.Lattice][] module made `Con рқ‘Ё` a meet-semilattice
under containment, with meet given by intersection (the tractable half of the
congruence lattice).  The join requires more work; it is the *least* congruence
containing the union; that is, the  **congruence generated by** the union.  This
module supplies the subsidiary result on which the join rests: for any binary
relation `R` on the carrier of `рқ‘Ё` there is a least congruence `Cg R` containing `R`.

We build `Cg R` as the inductively-defined closure `Gen R` of `R` under the
congruence-forming rules: it contains `R` (`base`), it contains the setoid equality
`_вүҲ_` (`rfl`), and it is closed under symmetry, transitivity, and the basic
operations (`symm`, `tran`, `comp`).  The following two facts make this *the*
generated congruence and constitute the **Congruence Generation Theorem**:

+  `Cg R` is a congruence containing `R` (so it is *an* upper bound of `R`); and
+  every congruence `ПҲ` containing `R` contains `Cg R` (so it is the *least* upper
   bound) вҖ” this is `Cg-least`, proved by induction on `Gen`.

From `Cg` we obtain the **join** `Оё вҲЁ ПҶ = Cg(Оё вҲӘ ПҶ)` and prove it is the least
upper bound of `Оё` and `ПҶ` in the containment order.  Because the closure quantifies
over the operations and the carrier, `Gen R` lands at level `рқ“һ вҠ” рқ“Ҙ вҠ” Оұ вҠ” ПҒ вҠ” в„“`
(not `в„“`); assembling this into a single-level `Lattice`/`CompleteLattice` bundle вҖ”
where that level is absorbed вҖ” is the remaining step of the congruence-lattice work
and is deferred to a follow-up.

```agda
{-# OPTIONS --cubical-compatible --exact-split --safe #-}

open import Overture using (рқ“һ ; рқ“Ҙ ; Signature)

module Setoid.Algebras.Congruences.Generation {рқ‘Ҷ : Signature рқ“һ рқ“Ҙ} where

open import Agda.Primitive using () renaming ( Set to Type )

-- Imports from the Agda Standard Library ---------------------------------------
open import Data.Product     using ( _,_ ; projвӮҒ ; projвӮӮ )
open import Data.Sum.Base    using ( _вҠҺ_ ; injвӮҒ ; injвӮӮ ; [_,_] )
open import Level            using ( Level ; _вҠ”_ )
open import Relation.Binary  using ( Setoid ; IsEquivalence )
                             renaming ( Rel to BinRel ; _вҮ’_ to _вҠҶ_)
-- Imports from the Agda Universal Algebras Library ------------------------------
open import Overture using ( вҲЈ_вҲЈ ; вҲҘ_вҲҘ )
open import Setoid.Algebras.Basic {рқ‘Ҷ = рқ‘Ҷ} using ( Algebra ; рқ•Ң[_] ; _^_ )
open import Setoid.Algebras.Congruences {рқ‘Ҷ = рқ‘Ҷ}
  using  ( Con ; mkcon ; _вҲЈвүҲ_ ; reflexive ; is-equivalence ; is-compatible )

private variable Оұ ПҒ в„“ в„“вҖІ : Level
```

#### Inductive Generation of a Congruence

Fix an algebra `рқ‘Ё` and a binary relation `R` on its carrier.  `Gen R` is the
smallest relation containing `R` that is reflexive over `_вүҲ_`, symmetric,
transitive, and compatible with every basic operation.  The closure quantifies over
the operation symbols (`рқ“һ`), their arities (`рқ“Ҙ`), and the carrier (`Оұ`, `ПҒ`), so it
inhabits `BinRel рқ•Ң[ рқ‘Ё ] (рқ“һ вҠ” рқ“Ҙ вҠ” Оұ вҠ” ПҒ вҠ” в„“)`; we name that level `рқ’Ҳ в„“`.

```agda
module _ {рқ‘Ё : Algebra Оұ ПҒ} where
  open Algebra рқ‘Ё using () renaming ( Domain to рқҗҖ )
  open Setoid рқҗҖ using ( _вүҲ_ ) renaming ( refl to reflA )

  -- The level at which the generated congruence lives.
  рқ’Ҳ : Level вҶ’ Level
  рқ’Ҳ в„“ = рқ“һ вҠ” рқ“Ҙ вҠ” Оұ вҠ” ПҒ вҠ” в„“

  data Gen (R : BinRel рқ•Ң[ рқ‘Ё ] в„“) : BinRel рқ•Ң[ рқ‘Ё ] (рқ’Ҳ в„“) where
    base : R вҠҶ Gen R
    rfl  : {x y : рқ•Ң[ рқ‘Ё ]} вҶ’ x вүҲ y вҶ’ Gen R x y
    symm : {x y : рқ•Ң[ рқ‘Ё ]} вҶ’ Gen R x y вҶ’ Gen R y x
    tran : {x y z : рқ•Ң[ рқ‘Ё ]} вҶ’ Gen R x y вҶ’ Gen R y z вҶ’ Gen R x z
    comp : (f : вҲЈ рқ‘Ҷ вҲЈ) {u v : вҲҘ рқ‘Ҷ вҲҘ f вҶ’ рқ•Ң[ рқ‘Ё ]}
      вҶ’ (вҲҖ i вҶ’ Gen R (u i) (v i)) вҶ’ Gen R ((f ^ рқ‘Ё) u) ((f ^ рқ‘Ё) v)

  Cg : (R : BinRel рқ•Ң[ рқ‘Ё ] в„“) вҶ’ Con рқ‘Ё {рқ’Ҳ в„“}
  Cg R = Gen R , mkcon rfl g-isEquivalence g-compatible
    where
    g-isEquivalence : IsEquivalence (Gen R)
    g-isEquivalence = record { refl = rfl reflA ; sym = symm ; trans = tran }

    g-compatible : рқ‘Ё вҲЈвүҲ Gen R
    g-compatible f h = comp f h
```

#### The Congruence Generation Theorem

`R` is contained in `Cg R` (the `base` constructor), and `Cg R` is the *least*
congruence with that property: any congruence `ПҲ` containing `R` already contains
`Gen R`.  The latter is proved by induction on the derivation of `Gen R x y`,
turning each closure rule into the corresponding congruence law of `ПҲ`
(`reflexive`, `sym`, `trans`, `is-compatible`).  Note `ПҲ` may live at any relation
level `в„“вҖІ`, so this is a genuinely heterogeneous statement.
вҠҶ
```agda
  Cg-incl : (R : BinRel рқ•Ң[ рқ‘Ё ] в„“) вҶ’ R вҠҶ Gen R
  Cg-incl R = base

  Cg-least : {R : BinRel рқ•Ң[ рқ‘Ё ] в„“} (ПҲ : Con рқ‘Ё {в„“вҖІ}) вҶ’ R вҠҶ projвӮҒ ПҲ вҶ’ Gen R вҠҶ projвӮҒ ПҲ
  Cg-least ПҲ RвҠҶПҲ (base r) = RвҠҶПҲ r
  Cg-least ПҲ RвҠҶПҲ (rfl e) = reflexive (projвӮӮ ПҲ) e
  Cg-least ПҲ RвҠҶПҲ (symm p) =
    IsEquivalence.sym (is-equivalence (projвӮӮ ПҲ)) (Cg-least ПҲ RвҠҶПҲ p)
  Cg-least ПҲ RвҠҶПҲ (tran p q) =
    IsEquivalence.trans (is-equivalence (projвӮӮ ПҲ)) (Cg-least ПҲ RвҠҶПҲ p) (Cg-least ПҲ RвҠҶПҲ q)
  Cg-least ПҲ RвҠҶПҲ (comp f h) = is-compatible (projвӮӮ ПҲ) f (О» i вҶ’ Cg-least ПҲ RвҠҶПҲ (h i))
```

Monotonicity follows immediately: if `R` is contained in `S` then `Cg R` is
contained in `Cg S` (take `ПҲ = Cg S`, which contains `S` hence `R`).

```agda
  Cg-mono : {R : BinRel рқ•Ң[ рқ‘Ё ] в„“} {S : BinRel рқ•Ң[ рқ‘Ё ] в„“вҖІ} вҶ’ R вҠҶ S вҶ’ Gen R вҠҶ Gen S
  Cg-mono {S = S} RвҠҶS = Cg-least (Cg S) (О» r вҶ’ base (RвҠҶS r))
```

#### The Join of Two Congruences

For congruences `Оё ПҶ : Con рқ‘Ё` the union `Оё вҲӘ ПҶ` of their underlying relations need
not be transitive, so we take the join to be the congruence it generates,
`Оё вҲЁ ПҶ = Cg(Оё вҲӘ ПҶ)`.  We record the order facts using a heterogeneous containment
`_вҠ‘_` (which coincides definitionally with the homogeneous `_вүӨ_` of
[Setoid.Algebras.Congruences.Lattice][] when the two levels agree), because the
join sits at the higher level `рқ’Ҳ в„“`.

```agda
  -- Heterogeneous containment of congruences.
  _вҠ‘_ : Con рқ‘Ё {в„“} вҶ’ Con рқ‘Ё {в„“вҖІ} вҶ’ Type (Оұ вҠ” в„“ вҠ” в„“вҖІ)
  Оё вҠ‘ ПҶ = projвӮҒ Оё вҠҶ projвӮҒ ПҶ
  infix 4 _вҠ‘_

  -- The union of the underlying relations of two congruences.
  _вҲӘбөЈ_ : Con рқ‘Ё {в„“} вҶ’ Con рқ‘Ё {в„“} вҶ’ BinRel рқ•Ң[ рқ‘Ё ] в„“
  (Оё вҲӘбөЈ ПҶ) x y = projвӮҒ Оё x y вҠҺ projвӮҒ ПҶ x y
  infixr 6 _вҲӘбөЈ_

  _вҲЁ_ : Con рқ‘Ё {в„“} вҶ’ Con рқ‘Ё {в„“} вҶ’ Con рқ‘Ё {рқ’Ҳ в„“}
  Оё вҲЁ ПҶ = Cg (Оё вҲӘбөЈ ПҶ)
  infixr 6 _вҲЁ_
```

The join is the least upper bound of its arguments: each argument is below it
(`base вҲҳ injвӮҒ`, `base вҲҳ injвӮӮ`), and it is below any common upper bound (by
`Cg-least`, since the union is below any congruence above both arguments).

```agda
  вҲЁ-upperЛЎ : {Оё ПҶ : Con рқ‘Ё {в„“}} вҶ’ Оё вҠ‘ (Оё вҲЁ ПҶ)
  вҲЁ-upperЛЎ p = base (injвӮҒ p)

  вҲЁ-upperКі : {Оё ПҶ : Con рқ‘Ё {в„“}} вҶ’ ПҶ вҠ‘ (Оё вҲЁ ПҶ)
  вҲЁ-upperКі q = base (injвӮӮ q)

  вҲЁ-least :  {Оё ПҶ : Con рқ‘Ё {в„“}} (ПҲ : Con рқ‘Ё {в„“вҖІ}) вҶ’ Оё вҠ‘ ПҲ вҶ’ ПҶ вҠ‘ ПҲ вҶ’ (Оё вҲЁ ПҶ) вҠ‘ ПҲ
  вҲЁ-least ПҲ ОёвҠ‘ПҲ ПҶвҠ‘ПҲ = Cg-least ПҲ (О» {x y} вҶ’ [ ОёвҠ‘ПҲ , ПҶвҠ‘ПҲ ])
```
